annotator_1.text: BTZSC is a comprehensive benchmark of 22 public datasets spanning sentiment, topic, intent, and emotion classification.
annotator_1.source_quote: we introduce BTZSC, a comprehensive benchmark of 22 public datasets spanning sentiment, topic, intent, and emotion classification

annotator_1.text: The study conducts a systematic comparison across four major model families: NLI cross-encoders, embedding models, rerankers and instruction-tuned LLMs, encompassing 38 public and custom checkpoints.
annotator_1.source_quote: we conduct a systematic comparison across four major model families, NLI cross-encoders, embedding models, rerankers and instruction-tuned LLMs, encompassing 38 public and custom checkpoints

annotator_1.text: Qwen3-Reranker-8B sets a new state-of-the-art with macro F1 = 0.72.
annotator_1.source_quote: modern rerankers, exemplified by Qwen3-Reranker-8B, set a new state-of-the-art with macro F1 = 0.72

annotator_1.text: Instruction-tuned LLMs at 4-12B parameters achieve macro F1 up to 0.67.
annotator_1.source_quote: instruction-tuned LLMs at 4--12B parameters achieve competitive performance (macro F1 up to 0.67)

annotator_1.text: Instruction-tuned LLMs excel particularly on topic classification but trail specialized rerankers.
annotator_1.source_quote: instruction-tuned LLMs at 4--12B parameters achieve competitive performance (macro F1 up to 0.67), excelling particularly on topic classification but trailing specialized rerankers

annotator_1.text: NLI cross-encoders plateau even as backbone size increases.
annotator_1.source_quote: NLI cross-encoders plateau even as backbone size increases

annotator_1.text: Scaling primarily benefits rerankers and LLMs over embedding models.
annotator_1.source_quote: scaling primarily benefits rerankers and LLMs over embedding models

annotator_2.text: Zero-shot text classification eliminates costly task-specific annotation by matching texts directly to human-readable label descriptions.
annotator_2.source_quote: Zero-shot text classification (ZSC) offers the promise of eliminating costly task-specific annotation by matching texts directly to human-readable label descriptions.

annotator_2.text: Early approaches to zero-shot text classification have predominantly relied on cross-encoder models fine-tuned for natural language inference.
annotator_2.source_quote: While early approaches have predominantly relied on cross-encoder models fine-tuned for natural language inference (NLI)

annotator_2.text: Recent advances in text-embedding models, rerankers, and instruction-tuned large language models have challenged the dominance of NLI-based architectures in zero-shot text classification.
annotator_2.source_quote: recent advances in text-embedding models, rerankers, and instruction-tuned large language models (LLMs) have challenged the dominance of NLI-based architectures.

annotator_2.text: Existing evaluations such as MTEB often incorporate labeled examples through supervised probes or fine-tuning.
annotator_2.source_quote: Existing evaluations, such as MTEB, often incorporate labeled examples through supervised probes or fine-tuning

annotator_2.text: Genuine zero-shot capabilities remain underexplored in existing evaluations.
annotator_2.source_quote: leaving genuine zero-shot capabilities underexplored.

annotator_2.text: BTZSC is a comprehensive benchmark of 22 public datasets.
annotator_2.source_quote: we introduce BTZSC, a comprehensive benchmark of 22 public datasets

annotator_2.text: BTZSC spans sentiment, topic, intent, and emotion classification tasks.
annotator_2.source_quote: spanning sentiment, topic, intent, and emotion classification

annotator_2.text: BTZSC captures diverse domains, class cardinalities, and document lengths.
annotator_2.source_quote: capturing diverse domains, class cardinalities, and document lengths.

annotator_2.text: The study conducts a systematic comparison across four major model families: NLI cross-encoders, embedding models, rerankers and instruction-tuned LLMs.
annotator_2.source_quote: we conduct a systematic comparison across four major model families, NLI cross-encoders, embedding models, rerankers and instruction-tuned LLMs

annotator_2.text: The study encompasses 38 public and custom checkpoints.
annotator_2.source_quote: encompassing 38 public and custom checkpoints.

annotator_2.text: Qwen3-Reranker-8B sets a new state-of-the-art with macro F1 = 0.72.
annotator_2.source_quote: modern rerankers, exemplified by Qwen3-Reranker-8B, set a new state-of-the-art with macro F1 = 0.72

annotator_2.text: Modern rerankers set a new state-of-the-art in zero-shot text classification.
annotator_2.source_quote: modern rerankers, exemplified by Qwen3-Reranker-8B, set a new state-of-the-art

annotator_2.text: GTE-large-en-v1.5 substantially closes the accuracy gap compared to other approaches.
annotator_2.source_quote: strong embedding models such as GTE-large-en-v1.5 substantially close the accuracy gap

annotator_2.text: Strong embedding models offer the best trade-off between accuracy and latency.
annotator_2.source_quote: strong embedding models such as GTE-large-en-v1.5 substantially close the accuracy gap while offering the best trade-off between accuracy and latency

annotator_2.text: Instruction-tuned LLMs at 4-12B parameters achieve competitive performance with macro F1 up to 0.67.
annotator_2.source_quote: instruction-tuned LLMs at 4--12B parameters achieve competitive performance (macro F1 up to 0.67)

annotator_2.text: Instruction-tuned LLMs excel particularly on topic classification.
annotator_2.source_quote: excelling particularly on topic classification

annotator_2.text: Instruction-tuned LLMs trail specialized rerankers in performance.
annotator_2.source_quote: but trailing specialized rerankers

annotator_2.text: NLI cross-encoders plateau even as backbone size increases.
annotator_2.source_quote: NLI cross-encoders plateau even as backbone size increases

annotator_2.text: Scaling primarily benefits rerankers and LLMs over embedding models.
annotator_2.source_quote: scaling primarily benefits rerankers and LLMs over embedding models.

annotator_2.text: BTZSC and accompanying evaluation code are publicly released.
annotator_2.source_quote: BTZSC and accompanying evaluation code are publicly released

annotator_2.text: BTZSC supports fair and reproducible progress in zero-shot text understanding.
annotator_2.source_quote: BTZSC and accompanying evaluation code are publicly released to support fair and reproducible progress in zero-shot text understanding.
